lorehq
─[2]-Primitives──

Active Work Architecture
─[ Documentation Restructuring ]─ ┐

Documentation Restructuring

Goal

Restructure documentation so operators and evaluators find what they need without wading through agent-internal operating procedures. Reduce cross-document duplication to prevent future drift.

Context

The 2026-02-11 assessments (second and fourth) identified documentation quality issues:

  1. Agent-facing docs presented as user docs: docs/operations/ describes what Lore should do internally (reconciliation checklists, skill creation workflows, hook behavior). These are essentially republished AGENTS.md content. An operator browsing the MkDocs site sees internal procedures alongside user guides.

  2. Cross-doc duplication: Several concepts are defined in 3-4 locations with slight variations:

    • Skill creation rules: AGENTS.md, operations/skill-creation.md, architecture/knowledge-and-growth.md, environment/conventions.md
    • Level prefixes: AGENTS.md, capabilities.md, conventions.md, multi-tier-architecture.md
    • Delegation model: AGENTS.md, delegation.md, how-it-works.md
    • Roadmap/plan definitions: AGENTS.md, roadmaps-and-plans.md, roadmaps/index.md, plans/index.md

  3. Guides section is a grab-bag: Mixes setup, concepts, reference, and comparison pages without subsections.

  4. Operational docs parity drift: Generated repos can miss pages linked from guides/examples, causing trust failures.

  5. Docs-to-code ratio imbalance (fourth assessment): 7,155 lines of docs governing 8,700 lines of functional code (near 1:1). 4,000 of those doc lines (56%) are work items (roadmaps, plans, archives). This signals over-governance to evaluators and new engineers. Archived plans alone account for ~1,940 lines across 20+ folders.

  6. Missing operator journey docs (fourth assessment): No “Day 1 after deployment” guide connecting setup to first interaction. No troubleshooting guide beyond sync issues. No guide for standalone-to-multi-tier migration. No /reconcile vs automatic hooks explanation.

  7. Quickstart broken (fourth assessment): guides/quickstart-5-minutes.md references bootstrap-standalone.sh but correct script is deploy-remote.sh. Breaks first-run experience.

  8. Confusing stubs (fourth assessment): docs/CLAUDE.md and docs/AGENTS.md are stubs saying “read the root file instead” — confusing for anyone browsing the docs site.

Approach

1. Audience separation for operations docs

Current state: docs/operations/ has 12 files presented flat under “Reference > Operations” in nav.

Target state: Split into two tiers in the nav:

  • Reference > How Lore Works Internally (relabel, add audience note)
    • Keep all current operations docs
    • Add a header note to the index: “These pages document Lore’s internal operating procedures. You don’t need to read them to use Lore — they’re published for transparency and for operators who want to understand or customize the agent’s behavior.”
    • This is a nav/labeling change, not a content move

Why not move files? Moving creates link churn. Relabeling the nav section and adding an audience note achieves the same clarity with less disruption.

2. Duplication reduction

For each duplicated concept, choose ONE canonical location. Other locations get a one-line summary + link.

ConceptCanonical LocationOther Locations (reduce to link)
Skill creation rulesoperations/skill-creation.mdAGENTS.md (keep — must be self-contained), conventions.md (reduce), knowledge-and-growth.md (reduce)
Level prefixesarchitecture/capabilities.mdconventions.md (reduce), multi-tier-architecture.md (reduce)
Delegation modeloperations/delegation.mdhow-it-works.md (keep summary, link for details)
Roadmap/plan definitionsguides/roadmaps-and-plans.mdroadmaps/index.md (reduce to link), plans/index.md (reduce to link)
Delegation decision treeoperations/delegation.mdhow-it-works.md (keep summary flowchart, link for details)
Skill naming conventionoperations/skill-creation.mdknowledge-and-growth.md (reduce), agent-creation.md (reduce)
Frontmatter formatexamples/roadmap-example.mdguides/roadmaps-and-plans.md (reduce to “see examples”)

Exception: AGENTS.md must remain self-contained (it’s the instruction file loaded every session). Duplication with AGENTS.md is acceptable and intentional.

2.5 Canonical map and drift watchlist

Create and maintain a canonical ownership map for high-impact concepts:

  • setup/onboarding -> guides/quickstart-5-minutes.md + getting-started.md
  • multi-tier model -> guides/multi-tier-architecture.md
  • contribution/sync workflow -> guides/commands.md + guides/sync-troubleshooting.md
  • agent internal behavior -> operations/

Then track known drift items explicitly until resolved:

  • docs claims that lag implementation semantics
  • config reference drift (opencode.json and runtime behavior)
  • nav entries that point to archived or moved work-plan paths

2.7 Work items and archive consolidation

Problem: 4,000 of 7,155 doc lines (56%) are work items. 20+ archived plans create nav bloat and signal over-governance.

Reduction target: Cut published docs volume by ~40% (from ~7,150 lines to ~4,300 lines). Primary lever is collapsing archived work items from individual folders to summary tables. Secondary lever is deduplication across overlapping concept explanations.

Approach:

  • Move archived plans out of published docs site (collapse to summary tables per roadmap)
  • Keep active plans visible; archive summaries link to git history for full detail
  • Separate framework development work from operator-facing content in nav

2.8 Fix broken references, stubs, and documentation gaps

  • Fix quickstart-5-minutes.md script reference (immediate, pilot-readiness gate)
  • Remove or fill docs/CLAUDE.md and docs/AGENTS.md stubs (either delete or embed content)
  • Add “Day 1 after deployment” bridging content between setup and interaction guides
  • Add reading order note to architecture docs (how-it-works → knowledge-and-growth → design-rationale → session-acceleration → capabilities)
  • Update docs/operations/skill-creation.md with explicit size exception: “Domain skills: 30-50 lines. Orchestrator workflows with multi-step algorithms: up to 200 lines with documented justification.” (6 orchestrator skills currently exceed the guideline without acknowledgment)
  • Update hooks/README.md to document all 5 hooks defined in settings.json (currently documents only 3; missing PreToolUse/protect-memory.js and PostToolUseFailure)

3. Guides reorganization

Current nav (flat, 12 items):

Guides:
  - 5-Minute Quickstart
  - Interaction
  - Commands
  - Roadmaps & Plans
  - Model Selection
  - Custom Skills & Agents
  - Lore vs Alternatives
  - Framework vs Dev Artifacts
  - Multi-Tier Architecture
  - Organization Deployment
  - Configuration Reference
  - Sync Troubleshooting

Target nav (grouped, same files):

Guides:
  - Getting Started:
      - 5-Minute Quickstart
      - Interaction
      - Lore vs Alternatives
  - Using Lore:
      - Commands
      - Roadmaps & Plans
      - Model Selection
      - Custom Skills & Agents
  - Multi-Tier:
      - Architecture
      - Organization Deployment
      - Sync Troubleshooting
  - Reference:
      - Configuration Reference
      - Framework vs Dev Artifacts

No files move. Only the mkdocs.yml nav structure changes.

4. Examples and parity policy

docs/examples/ is now a shipped docs surface, not source-only reference material.

  • Keep examples linked from roadmap/plan docs
  • Ensure operational generation includes docs/examples/
  • Do not introduce docs links that require level-specific conditionals unless unavoidable

When docs must differ by level, document the boundary explicitly and add a validation check.

Prior Work

This plan focuses on structural docs quality. Pre-pilot parity and CI gate work was completed and archived:

  • Generated Repo Parity Hardening (archived in lorehq-legacy)
  • CI Truth Gates (archived in lorehq-legacy)

Implementation Order

Phase 0 Pilot-Readiness Mandatory

  1. ✅ Fix trust-impacting drift (configuration-reference, OpenCode plugin semantics, hook coverage docs)
  2. ✅ Remove contribution guidance that conflicts with implementation (agents are non-contributable)
  3. ✅ Fix first-run trust-impacting doc breaks before pilot start
  4. ✅ Update create-skill/SKILL.md with skill split conventions (interaction method boundary, concern boundary, policy vs procedure decision tree)
  5. ✅ Update skill size guidance from 30-50 to 30-80 lines with split trigger at 80
  6. ✅ Reinforce environment-agnostic rule (no IPs, no doc cross-links in skills)
  7. ✅ Update docs/operations/skill-creation.md with split evaluation and updated size guidance
  8. ✅ Update docs/environment/conventions.md skill size line
  9. ✅ Add skill split audit to reconciliation checklist in AGENTS.md

Phase 2 Post-Pilot Restructuring

  1. Add audience note to operations/index.md and relabel in nav
  2. Reorganize guides nav into subsections
  3. Reduce duplication (one concept at a time, verify links after each)
  4. Run full link validation after all changes
  5. Add and maintain canonical-map section in docs architecture/operations references

Success Criteria

  • Operations docs have clear “internal reference” audience note
  • Guides nav has logical subsections (not flat list)
  • No concept defined in full in more than 2 locations (canonical + AGENTS.md)
  • All internal links pass validation after restructuring
  • Published docs volume reduced by ~40% (from ~7,150 to ~4,300 lines)
  • No content deleted — only restructured, deduplicated, and collapsed (full detail preserved in git history)
  • docs/examples/ is treated as a first-class shipped docs area with no broken references
  • Canonical concept map is published and referenced from operator-facing docs
  • Known drift watchlist is reduced to zero open high-impact items

Risks

RiskMitigation
Link breakage from nav changesNav-only changes don’t break links; file paths unchanged
Duplication reduction removes important contextKeep one-line summaries with links; don’t just delete
AGENTS.md deduplication breaks session loadingAGENTS.md is explicitly excluded from deduplication

Test Plan

bash scripts/validate-consistency.sh       # Full consistency check
bash scripts/validate-doc-claims.sh        # Claim validation
mkdocs build --strict 2>&1 | grep -i warn  # No MkDocs warnings
PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
Donate 0.24.4
